message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_affine_grid autograd via apply_function with linear backward [bd-bfj5]

tensor_affine_grid's forward is purely linear in theta:
    grid[n,h,w,0] = t00*x_w + t01*y_h + t02
    grid[n,h,w,1] = t10*x_w + t11*y_h + t12
where (x_w, y_h) are derived from output_size and align_corners
(no theta dependence). Backward is the same accumulator with
grad_grid in place of the constant 1:
    grad_theta[n,0,0] = sum_{h,w} grad_grid[n,h,w,0] * x_w
    grad_theta[n,0,1] = sum_{h,w} grad_grid[n,h,w,0] * y_h
    grad_theta[n,0,2] = sum_{h,w} grad_grid[n,h,w,0]
    grad_theta[n,1,*] = same with grad_grid[..,1]

Wrap the F64 path in tensor_apply_function with this analytical
backward. F32 theta with requires_grad=true still hits the
fail-loud guard (tensor_apply_function is f64-only and dtype
promotion would silently change F32 → F64 outputs).

Adds tensor_affine_grid_propagates_gradient_through_theta
regression test: identity theta on output_size [1, 1, 2, 2] with
align_corners=true. The axis coordinates are evenly-spaced
[-1, 1], so under sum loss grad_theta = [0, 0, 4, 0, 0, 4]
(translation channels see #cells=4, scale channels see
sum(±1) = 0).

Renamed the prior tensor_affine_grid_and_grid_sample_fail_closed_for_grad_inputs
test to tensor_grid_sample_fails_closed_for_grad_inputs and
removed the affine_grid half — that path now supports autograd.
grid_sample autograd is still tracked under the parent
frankentorch-3v6e umbrella (separate child bead needed).

Closes frankentorch-bfj5. ft-api lib tests: 1137 passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -61,6 +61,7 @@
 {"id":"frankentorch-b5of","title":"torch.special functions lack subprocess conformance: digamma, polygamma, logit, xlog1py, entr, multigammaln","description":"All torch.special.* functions in ft-api/lib.rs are hand-rolled approximations (digamma_approx, polygamma_approx, etc.) but lack subprocess conformance against scipy.special. The existing unit tests assert single known values within 1e-6 — too loose to catch silent precision regressions. Add subprocess conformance harnesses comparing each against scipy.special.{digamma, polygamma, logit, xlog1py, entr, multigammaln}. Pattern: matches the existing libm subprocess conformance tests (torch_erf_erfc_libm_subprocess_conformance etc.) — ctypes-from-libm or scipy-via-numpy oracle, ULP-tolerant comparison.","notes":"All slices added across recent commits: digamma (subprocess conformance done in earlier round), polygamma (b1012df), xlog1py (a3ea89a), entr (188aa4a), logit (284a0ab), multigammaln (this commit). Each test fail-loud against scipy.special with ULP / relative-tolerance comparators. Total ~150 inputs covered across the six harnesses.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-29T20:07:12.196533219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T21:51:45.349039993Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T21:51:45.345698845Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-b6n","title":"Audit ft-data loading path for latent correctness bugs","description":"## Context
The explicit parity backlog is empty, but we still need fresh-eyes subsystem audits to find latent correctness bugs hidden behind current green status. Start with ft-data and trace its batching/shuffle/drop_last behavior through tests and adjacent crate usage.

## Goal
Identify and fix any obvious correctness bug in the ft-data loading path, then verify with focused tests and workspace gates.

## Acceptance
- trace ft-data end-to-end
- fix at least one high-confidence correctness bug if found
- add/adjust tests proving the fix
- verify with rch cargo check/clippy/tests","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:10:45.588213207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:20:40.140473566Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:20:40.140223743Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-b8f","title":"ft-api: fail closed on out-of-range indexed write ops","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:40:21.732572151Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T21:43:18.086308785Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T21:43:18.085991304Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-bfj5","title":"affine_grid lacks autograd: linear in theta, easy backward [child of 3v6e]","description":"tensor_affine_grid currently has a fail-loud guard rejecting requires_grad=true theta. Math is purely linear in theta — for each (n, h, w) output cell:
    grid[n,h,w,0] = t00*x_w + t01*y_h + t02
    grid[n,h,w,1] = t10*x_w + t11*y_h + t12
where (x_w, y_h) are derived from output_size and align_corners (no theta dependence).

Backward (vector-Jacobian):
    grad_theta[n,0,0] = sum_{h,w} grad_grid[n,h,w,0] * x_w
    grad_theta[n,0,1] = sum_{h,w} grad_grid[n,h,w,0] * y_h
    grad_theta[n,0,2] = sum_{h,w} grad_grid[n,h,w,0]
    grad_theta[n,1,0] = sum_{h,w} grad_grid[n,h,w,1] * x_w
    grad_theta[n,1,1] = sum_{h,w} grad_grid[n,h,w,1] * y_h
    grad_theta[n,1,2] = sum_{h,w} grad_grid[n,h,w,1]

Wrap in tensor_apply_function with these accumulator loops in the backward closure. No saved tensors needed beyond the output_size + align_corners (captured by the closures). Standard PyTorch parity — torch.nn.functional.affine_grid supports backward.

Child of frankentorch-3v6e.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Added an autograd path inside tensor_affine_grid that wraps the linear forward + analytical backward in tensor_apply_function for F64 theta. F32 theta with requires_grad=true continues to fail-loud (preserves dtype semantics; tensor_apply_function is f64-only). Backward accumulates grad_theta from grad_grid by reapplying the (x_w, y_h) coordinates that the forward used. Added tensor_affine_grid_propagates_gradient_through_theta regression test in ft-api covering the analytical [0, 0, 4, 0, 0, 4] gradient under sum loss. Renamed the existing fail-closed test to tensor_grid_sample_fails_closed_for_grad_inputs (grid_sample autograd is still a separate child of frankentorch-3v6e). ft-api lib tests: 1137.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T22:30:24.096365129Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T22:33:12.434948653Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T22:33:12.432182704Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-c36b","title":"Linear algebra ops lack subprocess conformance: svd, qr, cholesky, det, slogdet, inv, pinverse, solve, eigh, lstsq, matrix_norm","description":"FEATURE_PARITY.md claims parity_green for these ops but none have subprocess conformance against numpy.linalg / scipy.linalg. Add diff-tests across well-conditioned and edge-case matrices (identity, scaled identity, singular, near-singular, complex eigenstructure). Pattern: matches torch_vector_norm_numpy_subprocess_conformance / torch_diff_numpy_subprocess_conformance — numpy oracle, ULP- or relative-tolerant comparison. Coverage: at least det / slogdet / inv / solve as the highest-impact subset; the others can follow incrementally.","notes":"All available linalg slices added across recent commits: det (earlier round), slogdet (e8cfc36), inv (75dbb04), solve (7115a2a), cholesky (ea904bb), qr (9a86171), svd (62ac52d), pinv (6e86432), eigh (a4b2758), lstsq (this commit). matrix_norm has no separate API in ft-api (vector_norm covers the existing surface and was already locked by torch_vector_norm_numpy_subprocess_conformance). Ten subprocess harnesses against numpy.linalg, all fail-loud, with structural-invariant comparisons for the sign-ambiguous decompositions (qr/svd/eigh) and element-wise comparisons for the unique outputs (det/slogdet/inv/solve/cholesky/pinv/lstsq). One real bug surfaced: SVD doesn't complete the orthonormal basis on rank-deficient inputs — tracked under frankentorch-zs8a.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-29T20:20:37.714643912Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T22:11:05.951874677Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T22:11:05.949322742Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-c3d","title":"Add CUDA/GPU execution backend","description":"FrankenTorch currently exposes `Device::Cuda` tags and device-guard validation, but all real execution remains CPU-only. There are no CUDA kernels, no device memory model, no dispatcher backend selection beyond CPU, and no runtime integration for GPU execution.

Scope for eventual closure:
1. Add concrete GPU storage/device abstractions beyond enum tagging
2. Implement CUDA dispatch keys and backend routing
3. Port core kernels (elementwise, reductions, matmul, indexing) to GPU
4. Preserve deterministic autograd/DAC evidence across cross-device execution
5. Add conformance and benchmark coverage for CPU↔GPU parity

This bead exists so FEATURE_PARITY.md can truthfully mark CUDA/GPU as deferred rather than silently omitted.","status":"deferred","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:23:50.059185557Z","created_by":"CopperStork","updated_at":"2026-04-28T03:33:49.271814166Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-c4tx","title":"masked_scatter all-false fast path drops source zero-gradient","description":"Reviewer finding in crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs. The current tensor_masked_scatter implementation returns Ok(input) immediately when n_true == 0 at lines 11682-11688. That preserves input passthrough but removes the source tensor from the backward graph entirely. The adjacent regression test masked_scatter_all_false_mask_passes_input_through_with_grad at lines 33742-33761 says source gradient should be zero, but it only asserts input_grad, so the missing source gradient is not covered. Repro by code inspection: create input requires_grad=true, all-zero mask, source requires_grad=true, call tensor_masked_scatter, sum the output, run tensor_backward, then query tensor_gradient(report, source). Because the fast path returns the original input node, compute_reachable in ft-autograd only reaches input and tensor_gradient(source) is None instead of a zero vector of source.numel(). This silently violates the deterministic autograd contract for a tracked source argument. Fix by composing the all-false case through an autograd-aware zero dependency on source, or by adding an explicit masked_scatter op/backward rule that records source and emits zero source gradients when no mask entries consume it. Add a regression assertion that all-false masks produce source_grad == vec![0.0; source_numel].","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:00:50.510404660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T18:34:57.725416487Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T18:34:57.723645605Z","close_reason":"Removed the n_true==0 fast-path return-input shortcut for non-empty source — the gather + where composition now runs unconditionally for source_numel >= 1, so source stays in the backward graph and tensor_gradient(source) returns zeros(source.shape) instead of None. Empty-source case kept as a fast path since its gradient is shape-empty regardless. Added masked_scatter_all_false_mask_with_empty_source_returns_input edge-case test and strengthened masked_scatter_all_false_mask_passes_input_through_with_grad to assert source_grad == zeros.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-eng","title":"Harden state dict tensor-count bounds","description":"load_state_dict_from_bytes should reject impossible tensor counts early to avoid excessive loops/DoS; add regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T19:27:41.404491540Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T19:27:45.344006893Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T19:27:45.343697838Z","close_reason":"Bounded state dict tensor counts with min-bytes check; added regression test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-etu","title":"Investigate and close next verified parity gap from FEATURE_PARITY","description":"No open beads remain. Use current source + FEATURE_PARITY + remote quality gates to identify one still-missing or broken FrankenTorch parity surface, implement it, verify it, and sync beads.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-01T20:33:53.342830285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T20:42:25.194397110Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T20:42:25.194152728Z","close_reason":"Added conformance coverage for cov/corrcoef/mode/quantile in tensor_advanced fixtures and runner; verified targeted ft-conformance test passes remotely; workspace check passes remotely; remaining fmt/clippy failures tracked separately.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-f6nn","title":"ft-nn PReLU and shrink activations sever autograd","description":"Reviewer finding in crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs after the recent activation-gradient repair wave. Several activation modules still read tensor values, compute in plain f64, and return session.tensor_variable(..., false), so a requires_grad=true input produces a fresh untracked output and tensor_backward cannot propagate gradients. Concrete sites: PReLU::forward lines 3576-3642 reads both input and its learnable weight via tensor_values and returns an untracked leaf, so both input gradients and the PReLU weight gradient are dropped; Softshrink lines 3918-3938, Hardshrink lines 3966-3978, Tanhshrink lines 3991-4000, Softsign lines 4014-4023, Softmin lines 4046-4057, and RReLU lines 4154-4168 have the same severed-input pattern. Existing tests for these modules are forward-only (for example prelu_default_forward/prelu_has_parameters/prelu_multichannel, softshrink_shrinks, tanhshrink_values, softsign_bounded, softmin_is_softmax_of_negated, rrelu_module_*), and the full ft-nn test suite still passes: rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-nn reported 508 passed on 2026-04-29. Repro by code inspection: create a requires_grad=true input, run PReLU::forward (or any listed activation), reduce with tensor_sum, then tensor_backward; tensor_gradient for input is absent because the forward output is a requires_grad=false leaf. For PReLU, tensor_gradient for self.weight is also absent even though parameters() exposes that learnable slope. Fix by composing each activation through existing autograd-aware primitives (e.g. PReLU via tensor_where/tensor_mul with broadcasted weight; shrink ops via tensor_where; Softmin via tensor_neg or 0-input subtraction then tensor_softmax; RReLU via leaky_relu-style composition) and add regression tests asserting input gradients, plus weight gradients for PReLU.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:09:37.892015821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T18:53:11.745564147Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T18:53:11.743783997Z","close_reason":"Composed all 7 listed activation modules through autograd-aware primitives: PReLU via tensor_reshape+tensor_expand+tensor_mul+tensor_where (now propagates to both input and learnable weight); Softshrink/Hardshrink via nested tensor_where + tensor_abs/tensor_sub/tensor_add + tensor_gt/tensor_lt; Tanhshrink via tensor_sub(input, tanh(input)); Softsign via tensor_div(input, 1+abs); Softmin via tensor_neg + tensor_softmax; RReLU via where(x>=0, x, slope*x). Added 8 gradient regression tests asserting analytical d/dx for each module (plus PReLU weight grads, both single-slope and multichannel). All 11 existing forward tests continue to pass — forward parity preserved.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id": "frankentorch-fntr", "title": "softmax / log_softmax lack subprocess conformance against torch/numpy", "description": "tensor_softmax and tensor_log_softmax are the canonical activation/output functions for classification heads, attention layers, and most loss formulations. They have unit tests in ft-api but no subprocess conformance against an authoritative reference (torch.nn.functional.softmax / scipy.special.softmax / numpy). Bit-exact agreement matters because:

- Numerical stability: log_softmax(x) = log(softmax(x)) is computed via the max-subtraction trick to avoid exp overflow; the equivalence with the naive form must hold within libm precision across all inputs.
- Gradient stability: softmax/log_softmax forward/backward are coupled (dL/dx = softmax(x) - target for cross-entropy). Drift between FrankenTorch and PyTorch here corrupts every classification training run.
- Edge cases: large positive inputs (exp overflow risk), large negative (underflow), uniform input (all equal probabilities), single-element softmax (degenerate).

Add torch_softmax_torch_subprocess_conformance covering interior, large-magnitude, near-uniform, and edge-case inputs along multiple dims; assert ULP-tolerant equality against scipy.special.softmax (or numpy formulation if scipy unavailable). Same for log_softmax.

Bias toward filing-from-/reality-check: this is a pre-existing instance test gap, not a new bug.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-29T22:22:44.964172677Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-29T22:27:34.202656Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "closed_at": "2026-04-29T22:27:34.202656Z", "close_reason": "Closed by torch_softmax_scipy_subprocess_conformance landed in 155daa1 alongside the log_softmax precision fix (frankentorch-ebrb). The conformance harness covers 9 cases across 1-D / 2-D-along-dim0 / 2-D-along-dim1 / 3-D-middle-dim shapes including large-magnitude inputs that surfaced the precision bug."}
+{"id":"frankentorch-fntr","title":"softmax / log_softmax lack subprocess conformance against torch/numpy","description":"tensor_softmax and tensor_log_softmax are the canonical activation/output functions for classification heads, attention layers, and most loss formulations. They have unit tests in ft-api but no subprocess conformance against an authoritative reference (torch.nn.functional.softmax / scipy.special.softmax / numpy). Bit-exact agreement matters because:

- Numerical stability: log_softmax(x) = log(softmax(x)) is computed via the max-subtraction trick to avoid exp overflow; the equivalence with the naive form must hold within libm precision across all inputs.
- Gradient stability: softmax/log_softmax forward/backward are coupled (dL/dx = softmax(x) - target for cross-entropy). Drift between FrankenTorch and PyTorch here corrupts every classification training run.
- Edge cases: large positive inputs (exp overflow risk), large negative (underflow), uniform input (all equal probabilities), single-element softmax (degenerate).

Add torch_softmax_torch_subprocess_conformance covering interior, large-magnitude, near-uniform, and edge-case inputs along multiple dims; assert ULP-tolerant equality against scipy.special.softmax (or numpy formulation if scipy unavailable). Same for log_softmax.

Bias toward filing-from-/reality-check: this is a pre-existing instance test gap, not a new bug.","notes":"Closed by adding torch_softmax_scipy_subprocess_conformance covering interior, uniform, scalar, large-positive / large-negative (with max-subtract numerical stability), mixed-sign, and 1-D / 2-D / 3-D shapes across multiple dims. 8-ULP tolerance with 1e-15 absolute floor. Also surfaced the log_softmax precision bug tracked under frankentorch-ebrb (now fixed in the same commit).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-29T22:22:44.964172677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T22:26:01.625884901Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T22:26:01.622244776Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-fq9","title":"Add trapezoid, cumulative_trapezoid, addr, inner to ft-api","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:31:01.289432681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:32:53.881175699Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:32:53.880929192Z","close_reason":"Implemented trapezoid, cumulative_trapezoid, addr, inner with 9 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-g4c","title":"Add conformance coverage for deterministic random tensor ops","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:12:01.494480746Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:39.943682953Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T20:20:39.943365522Z","close_reason":"Added dedicated tensor_random conformance fixture family and smoke coverage; verified targeted ft-conformance test/check/clippy via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-g7f","title":"ft-data dataloader initial shuffle parity","description":"DataLoader with DataLoaderConfig::with_shuffle(true) preserves sequential order on first epoch until reset() is called. PyTorch-observable behavior expects the first epoch to be shuffled as well. Fix construction-time behavior and add regression coverage in ft-data.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:24:12.805969180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:34:13.512441665Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T01:34:13.512255252Z","close_reason":"Implemented first-epoch shuffle parity and regression coverage in ft-data","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
